aliases: Risotto with Taleggio cheese and sausage

english: Risotto with Taleggio cheese and sausage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'risotto' class (FOODON_00002694); no specific Taleggio risotto entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers risotto as a generic rice dish category; no specific Taleggio variant code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for risotto al Taleggio. No suitable parent concept identified for this prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of risotto al Taleggio is approximately 350 g, presented as a single plate of creamy rice topped with melted Taleggio cheese and crumbled pork sausage.

nutrient_source.title: Risotto with sausage and cheese — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Risotto (cooked Carnaroli rice base), Italian pork sausage, and Taleggio-type soft cheese
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for risotto al Taleggio. Values are estimated by weighted-average composition of three components: (1) cooked Carnaroli-style risotto base (~60% by weight, USDA SR Legacy 'Rice, white, short-grain, cooked' FDC ID 168878 as proxy), (2) Italian pork sausage (~20% by weight, USDA FDC ID 167932 'Pork sausage, Italian, cooked'), and (3) Taleggio-type washed-rind soft cheese (~20% by weight, USDA FDC ID 173420 'Cheese, Brie' used as closest soft-ripened cheese proxy given absence of Taleggio entry; Taleggio has comparable fat and protein profile). Butter and aromatics contribute minor macro adjustments. Values represent a plausible per-100g estimate for a restaurant-style preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for risotto (Arborio/short-grain white rice cooked with fat and protein)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for risotto al Taleggio. GI estimated at 54 based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for risotto made with Arborio/short-grain white rice (GI range 54–69 depending on fat and protein content). The presence of significant fat (Taleggio cheese, butter) and protein (sausage, cheese) substantially lowers the effective GI of the rice component through delayed gastric emptying. A conservative estimate of GI 54 (low end of the risotto range) is used, consistent with the high fat and protein content of this preparation. gi_category set to 'low' per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55). Glycemic load per 350 g serving = (54 × 18.5 × 3.5) / 100 = 35.0.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from Taleggio cheese, Italian pork sausage, and added stock
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size of risotto al Taleggio due to its estimated 420 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 1470 mg per restaurant serving), which represents a substantial proportion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI ~54) with significant carbohydrate load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the GI of risotto al Taleggio is estimated at 54 (low category), the high carbohydrate content per restaurant serving (~65 g per 350 g plate) produces a substantial glycemic load of approximately 35, and individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size carefully and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat from Taleggio cheese and pork sausage, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Risotto al Taleggio is high in saturated fat (~5.1 g per 100 g, approximately 18 g per serving) and sodium (~1470 mg per serving), both of which are established risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with CVD or dyslipidaemia should treat this dish as an occasional indulgence and limit serving frequency.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Risotto al Taleggio is a classic northern Italian rice dish originating from the Lombardy region, where Taleggio cheese — a semi-soft, washed-rind cow's milk cheese with a pungent aroma and buttery, tangy flavour — is the defining ingredient. The dish is prepared using Carnaroli rice, prized for its high starch content and ability to absorb liquid while retaining a firm centre, which is toasted in butter with finely diced onion before being gradually hydrated with warm stock and white wine using the traditional risotto method of continuous stirring. Crumbled Italian pork sausage (salsiccia) is cooked separately and folded in during the final stages, adding savoury depth and richness. Taleggio cheese is stirred in off the heat during the mantecatura step, melting into the rice to create a luxuriously creamy, cohesive texture. The finished dish is soft, glossy, and intensely flavoured, with a balance of starchy creaminess, meaty savoriness, and the characteristic mild funk of the Taleggio. Nutritionally, risotto al Taleggio is energy-dense at approximately 185 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein (~8 g) from the sausage and cheese, moderate carbohydrate (~18.5 g) from the rice, and significant fat (~9.5 g) predominantly saturated from the dairy and pork components. Sodium content is elevated (~420 mg/100 g) due to the cheese, sausage, and stock. The glycemic index is estimated at 54 (low category), moderated by the fat and protein content slowing gastric emptying. Clinically, this dish warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sodium, saturated fat, and substantial glycemic load per restaurant serving. It is not suitable for halal, kosher, or dairy-free diets.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for risotto al Taleggio as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for cooked short-grain rice, Italian pork sausage, and a soft-ripened cheese proxy (Brie used as closest available match for Taleggio). GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database risotto range. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition of three USDA FoodData Central components: cooked short-grain white rice (60% weight), Italian pork sausage cooked (20% weight), and Brie-type soft cheese as Taleggio proxy (20% weight). Taleggio is not present in USDA FoodData Central; Brie (FDC ID 173420) is used as the closest available soft-ripened cow's milk cheese with comparable fat (~28 g/100g) and protein (~20 g/100g) profile. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for estimation. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported consistently across the component entries used for estimation. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy rice and cheese component entries used. 5. GI estimated at 54 based on University of Sydney GI Database risotto entries; gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. 6. Glycemic load per 350 g serving calculated as (54 × 18.5 × 3.5) / 100 = 35.0. 7. This dish contains pork sausage and is not halal or kosher. 8. Allergens include milk (Taleggio cheese, butter, Parmigiano) and potentially gluten (from stock or sausage casing). 9. This is an Italian cuisine dish; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese cuisines do not apply. Local names are not required for Italian cuisine under Project Veritas v2 multilingual rules.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full nutrient profile via USDA FoodData Central weighted-average component estimation (rice base 60%, Italian pork sausage 20%, soft cheese proxy 20%).
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-absence justification in notes.
Estimated GI at 54 from University of Sydney GI Database risotto range, applied ≤55 threshold rule to set gi_category to 'low'.
Selected FoodOn FOODON_00002694 and FoodEx2 A00LC as closest_parent ontology anchors with gap notes; set SNOMED CT to null/none with gap note.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency — all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases.
Confirmed Italian cuisine does not trigger mandatory non-English multilingual naming requirements under G6.
Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance including quantified sodium and saturated fat per serving.
